# Name: # OTA-capable layout on 4 MB flash. Two equal app slots (ota_0/ota_1)
# of 1472 KB each — sized to fit the WiFi-enabled build (~1.3 MB)
# with ~10 % headroom. Previously 1280 KB which overflowed as soon
# as the Ethernet default flipped to WiFi. Storage partition keeps
# the "storage" label so announcement.c continues to mount it.
# The "pairing" partition (4 KB
# only by the browser-side flasher: it carries a per-install random
# secret that lets the install page locate the freshly-flashed dongle
# on the LAN without relying on mDNS / DHCP host-name. OTA-only dongles
# leave it 0xFF-erased and the firmware silently skips the pairing
# handshake.
# The "coredump" partition (52 KB) sits in the alignment gap between
# pairing (ends at 0x13000) and ota_0 (must start at the next 64 KB
# boundary
Type: 0x20000). The ESP32 panic handler writes an ELF-formatted

# Name: # core dump there on crash; on the next boot
# to the PhoneBlock backend and erases the slot. Dongles flashed with
# the older layout don't have this partition — esp_core_dump_image_check
# returns ESP_ERR_NOT_FOUND there and the upload path is skipped.
# One-way migration: existing dongles running the old factory-only
# layout must be reflashed over USB once. From there on
# flip between ota_0 and ota_1 automatically.
nvs
otadata
phy_init
pairing
coredump
ota_0
ota_1
storage
